message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  This paper presents the first analytical model for calculating how many tests and tracing needed to suppress COVID-19 transmission. The number of people needs to be tested daily is given by:
                  <jats:disp-formula id="ueqn1">
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="20120568v3_ueqn1.gif" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:disp-formula>
                  Where
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  is the size of the population in consideration
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  <jats:italic>A</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>r</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  is the attack rate at any given time
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  <jats:italic>T</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>p</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  is the test-positive rate
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  ρ is the percentage of infectious people that have to be detected per day. To make the effective reproduction number
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>e</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  below 1,
                  <jats:italic>ρ</jats:italic>
                  must satisfy the following equation:
                  <jats:disp-formula id="ueqn2">
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="20120568v3_ueqn2.gif" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:disp-formula>
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>Where</jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>0</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  is the basic reproduction number,
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>S/N is the percentage of the susceptible population over the entire population,</jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  <jats:italic>D</jats:italic>
                  is the length of the infectious period, and
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  <jats:italic>η</jats:italic>
                  is the percentage of close contacts that have to be traced.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>This model provides insights and guidance to deploy the testing and tracing resources optimally. An Excel model is attached to facilitate easy calculation of the number of tests and tracing needed. This model is also applicable to any infectious disease that can be suppressed by testing and tracing.</jats:p>
title: A Model for the Testing and Tracing Needed to Suppress COVID-19

reference.unstructured: Fred Brauer , Carlos Castillo-Chavez , and Zhilan Feng . Mathematical Models in Epidemiology (Texts in Applied Mathematics Book 69). Springer 2019
Arnold Bosman . Incubation period, Latent period and Generation time. Field of Epidemiology Manual Wiki. https://wiki.ecdc.europa.eu/fem/Pages/Incubationperiod,LatentperiodandGenerationtime..aspx

Ying Liu , Albert A. Gayle , Annelies Wilder-Smith , and Joacim Rocklöv . The reproductive number of COVID-19 is higher compared to SARS coronavirus. Journal of Travel Medicine, Volume 27, Issue 2, March 2020. https://academic.oup.com/jtm/article/27/2/taaa021/5735319

Leung N , Chu D , Shiu E , Chan KH , McDevitt J , Hau B et al. Respiratory virus shedding in exhaled breath and efficacy of face masks. Nature Medicine, April 3 2020. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41591-020-0843-2
Luís M. A. Bettencourt , Ruy M. Ribeiro . Real Time Bayesian Estimation of the Epidemic Potential of Emerging Infectious Diseases. PLOS ONE. May 14, 2008 https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0002185

Lauer S , Grantz K , Bi Q , Jones F. The Incubation Period of Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) From Publicly Reported Confirmed Cases: Estimation and Application. March 10, 2020. Annals of Internal Medicine https://www.acpjournals.org/doi/10.7326/M20-0504

Backer JA , Klingkenberg D , Wallinga J. Incubation period of 2019 novel coronavirus (2019-nCoV) infections among travelers from Wuhan, China, 20-28 January 2020. Euro Surveill. 2020 Feb;25(5). https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/32046819?dopt=Abstract

Position Statement from the National Centre for Infectious Diseases and the Chapter of Infectious Disease Physicians. Academy of Medicine, Singapore. May 23, 2020. Singapore study on infectious period

Liu Y , Yan L , Wan L , Xiang T , Le A , Liu J et al. Viral dynamics in mild and severe cases of COVID-19. Lancet, March 19, 2020. https://www.thelancet.com/journals/laninf/article/PIIS1473-3099(20)30232-2/fulltext

reference.article-title: Temporary Dynamics in Viral Shedding and Transmissibility of COVID-19
